symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic bladder pain, worst on bladder filling and voiding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bladder-filling and voiding-related suprapubic pain is the hallmark of IC/BPS.
Constant suprapubic bladder pain is not the unilateral colicky loin-to-groin pattern of ureteric colic.
Dysuria is common in IC/BPS flares (urothelial irritation).
Dysuria supports lower UTI — non-discriminating between infective and non-infective cystitis on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urinary frequency and urgency with small-volume voids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequency/urgency with small volumes is a core feature of IC/BPS.
Continued small-volume voiding excludes complete retention.
Absence of fever/rigors is a key feature arguing against pyelonephritis/urosepsis on clinical grounds.
A sterile, afebrile bladder pain picture is consistent with a non-infective IC/BPS flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: No flank/loin or costovertebral radiation of pain (patient-reportable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of loin/flank pain argues against upper-tract infection.
No loin-to-groin colicky radiation makes an obstructing ureteric stone unlikely.
Central sensitisation with allodynia is characteristic of chronic IC/BPS pain and explains disproportionate pain intensity.
Poor response to simple analgesia and urinary alkalinisers is typical of central/neuropathic bladder pain rather than a simple infection.
Ural (alkaliniser) failing to help is weakly against a straightforward lower UTI flare, though non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known prior diagnosis of interstitial cystitis / bladder pain syndrome with recurrent flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An established diagnosis with a recurrent flare pattern strongly raises the pre-test probability that this episode is another flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Maximum-dose OTC analgesia taken with desperation / accidental-overdose risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Does not change the bladder diagnosis, but is a critical parallel safety finding (paracetamol overdose risk + psychological distress) that must be assessed and managed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 110), tachypnoea (RR 24) and hypertension (145/95) — clinician-measured vitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain- and anxiety-driven sympathetic response with a normal temperature and preserved perfusion — NOT sepsis. Sealed clinician measurement; the AI should infer distress from patient-reported palpitations/fast breathing, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic tenderness/hyperaesthesia on palpation, no costovertebral-angle tenderness, non-distended bladder, external genitalia normal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Suprapubic tenderness with an absent CVA tenderness and non-distended bladder supports a bladder-localised flare over pyelonephritis or retention — sealed clinician exam finding.
Absence of costovertebral-angle tenderness argues against upper-tract infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine dipstick: negative nitrites, negative leukocytes, trace haematuria

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative nitrites and leukocytes with only trace blood — sterile pattern; formal MSU culture expected to return no significant growth, confirming a non-infective flare.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without confirming sterility, a treatable bacterial UTI could be missed, or (conversely) antibiotics could be given inappropriately for a sterile flare.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Negative nitrites and leukocytes make bacterial infection less likely (though dipstick is imperfect — MSU culture confirms).
A sterile urinalysis in a symptomatic bladder is consistent with a non-infective IC/BPS flare.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Suprapubic bladder-filling pain + frequency/urgency + afebrile + analgesia-resistant + known IC diagnosis + sterile urinalysis together form a high-probability non-infective flare pattern that should NOT be escalated to emergency care.
Afebrile + no flank/loin radiation + no costovertebral tenderness collectively exclude pyelonephritis clinically — the negative screen that justifies a non-emergency tier.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick + MSU microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms sterility (or identifies infection) before any antibiotic decision — the single investigation that changes management in this presentation. Appropriate to arrange promptly via GP.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum paracetamol level and LFTs (if maximum/uncertain paracetamol dose exceeded)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given maximum-dose paracetamol with desperation and a stated urge to take more, quantify the 24-hour dose; if the ingested amount is uncertain or above the maximum, a paracetamol level (timed) and LFTs are needed to exclude hepatotoxic overdose.

Not first-line here — the pain is constant suprapubic without colicky loin-to-groin features and there is no fever; routine imaging would be over-investigation for a typical IC/BPS flare with a negative stone/infection screen.
